title: Genome editing in the choanoflagellate Salpingoeca rosetta
author: David Booth
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bx58pq9w

steps.title: Seed a large culture of S
steps.instruction: Seed a large culture of S. rosetta .

steps.title: Two days prior to transfection, inoculate...
steps.instruction: Two days prior to transfection, inoculate 120 mL of high nutrient media with a culture of S. rosetta feeding on E. pacifica to a final concentration of S. rosetta of 8000 cells/ml . Salpingoeca rosetta cultured with Echinicola pacifica (SrEpac) ATCC Catalog #PRA-390

steps.title: Grow the culture for 48:00:00 in...
steps.instruction: Grow the culture for 48:00:00 in a 3-layer flask at 22 °C, with 60% humidity . Falcon 525cm² Rectangular Straight Neck Cell Culture Multi-Flask, 3-layer with Vented Cap Corning Catalog #353143

steps.title: Prepare a guide RNA (gRNA) that...
steps.instruction: Prepare a guide RNA (gRNA) that binds to Sp Cas9 and targets DNA by annealing CRISPR RNA (crRNA) with the trans-activating CRISPR RNA (tracrRNA) .

steps.title: Resuspend crRNA in duplex buffer (30 mM HEPES-KOH, pH 7
steps.instruction: Resuspend crRNA in duplex buffer (30 mM HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 100 mM potassium acetate) to a final concentration of 200 micromolar (µM) . crRNA Integrated DNA Technologies, Inc. (IDT) Catalog #Custom Order Duplex Buffer Integrated DNA Technologies, Inc. (IDT) Catalog #11-01-03-01

steps.title: Resuspend tracrRNA in duplex buffer to...
steps.instruction: Resuspend tracrRNA in duplex buffer to a final concentration of 200 micromolar (µM) . tracrRNA Integrated DNA Technologies, Inc. (IDT) Catalog #1072534

steps.title: Mix equal volumes of crRNA ( go to step #2
steps.instruction: Mix equal volumes of crRNA ( go to step #2.1 ) and tracrRNA ( go to step #2.2 ) to have a final concentration of 100 micromolar (µM) gRNA, which is the annealed complex of crRNA and tracrRNA.

steps.title: Incubate the gRNA solution at 95...
steps.instruction: Incubate the gRNA solution at 95 °C in an aluminum block for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place the aluminum block was placed...
steps.instruction: Place the aluminum block was placed at Room temperature to slowly cool the gRNA to 25 °C .

steps.title: Store the gRNA at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Store the gRNA at -20 °C .

steps.title: Prepare DNA oligonucleotides that serve as...
steps.instruction: Prepare DNA oligonucleotides that serve as repair templates after Sp Cas9 cleavage.

steps.title: Dissolve oligonucleotides to a final concentration...
steps.instruction: Dissolve oligonucleotides to a final concentration of 250 micromolar (µM) in 10 mM HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5. DNA Oligonucleotide Integrated DNA Technologies, Inc. (IDT) Catalog #Custom Order

steps.title: Incubate the dissolved oligonucleotides at 55 °C for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the dissolved oligonucleotides at 55 °C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Store oligonucleotides at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Store oligonucleotides at -20 °C .

steps.title: Before starting nucleofections, ensure that the...
steps.instruction: Before starting nucleofections, ensure that the oligonucleotides are fully dissolved by incubating them at 55 °C for 01:00:00 , which concurs with the assembly of the Sp Cas9/gRNA complex.

steps.title: Assemble Sp Cas9 with the gRNA...
steps.instruction: Assemble Sp Cas9 with the gRNA to form the Sp Cas9 RNP.

steps.title: For one transfection, place 2 µL...
steps.instruction: For one transfection, place 2 µL of 20 micromolar (µM), SpCas9 in the bottom of a 0.2 ml PCR tube. EnGen Cas9 NLS, S. pyogenes - 400 pmol New England Biolabs Catalog #M0646T

steps.title: Add 2 µL of 100 micromolar...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of 100 micromolar (µM), gRNA ( go to step #2 ) by slowly pipetting up and down with Sp Cas9 to gently mix the gRNA together. This solution is called the " Sp Cas9 ribonucleoprotein (RNP)."

steps.title: Incubate the Sp Cas9 RNP at...
steps.instruction: Incubate the Sp Cas9 RNP at Room temperature for 01:00:00 (roughly the time to complete the preparation of S. rosetta for priming, see below).

steps.title: Prepare SF Buffer (Lonza) for transfections
steps.instruction: Prepare SF Buffer (Lonza) for transfections. SF Cell Line 96-well Nucleofector Kit Lonza Catalog #V4SC-2096

steps.title: Add all of buffer B (smaller...
steps.instruction: Add all of buffer B (smaller volume that may also be called supplement 1) to buffer A (larger volume).

steps.title: Store on ice until ready for use
steps.instruction: Store on ice until ready for use. The combined buffer can also be stored at 4°C for up to 3 months. The combined buffer can be stored at 4°C for up to 3 months. Because the Lonza kits can be so expensive, we recommend aliquoting large volumes of the SF components (900 µl aliquots for buffer A and 200 µl aliquots for buffer B) to prevent SF buffer from spoiling after buffers A and B have been combined.

steps.title: Prepare the priming buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare the priming buffer.

steps.title: Dilute papain to a final concentration...
steps.instruction: Dilute papain to a final concentration of 100 µM in dilution buffer(50 mM HEPES-KOH pH 7.5, 200 mM sodium chloride, 20% [v/v] glycerol, and 10 mM cysteine) from a stock solution of 1 mM papain (Millipore Sigma, St. Louis, MO; Cat. No. P3125-100MG]), and incubate at room temperature just before priming cells for transfection. Papain from papaya latex Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P3125-100MG The dilution buffer [50 mM HEPES-KOH pH 7.5, 200 mM sodium chloride, 20% (v/v) glycerol and 10 mM cysteine] should be sterile filtered through a 0.22 µm filter. The dilution buffer may also be prepared ahead of time and stored in a -80°C freezer just before its use.

steps.title: Make a solution of the remaining...
steps.instruction: Make a solution of the remaining components of the priming buffer (40 mM HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 34 mM lithium citrate; 50 mM L-cysteine; 15% [wt/vol] PEG 8000). DO NOT combine the papain and priming buffer unti just before adding the priming buffer to cells. The priming buffer without papain should be sterile filtered through a 0.22 µm filter. The priming buffer without papain can also be made ahead of time and stored at -80°C until it is used. Be sure that the priming before is warmed to room temperature prior to use.

steps.title: Prepare S
steps.instruction: Prepare S. rosetta for transfection by washing away feeder bacteria.

steps.title: Homogenized the 120 mL culture of S
steps.instruction: Homogenized the 120 mL culture of S. rosetta feeding on E. pacifica ( go to step #1 ) by vigorously shaking and then split into 40 mL aliquots in 50 ml conical tubes.

steps.title: Vigorously shake the aliquots and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Vigorously shake the aliquots and centrifuge the cells for 00:05:00 at 2000 x g and 22 °C in a swinging bucket rotor.

steps.title: Use a serological pipette to gently...
steps.instruction: Use a serological pipette to gently remove from the cell pellet all but 2 ml of the supernatant, which remains cloudy with E. pacifica bacteria. With a fine tip transfer pipette, gently remove the remaining liquid near the pellet.

steps.title: The three cell pellets were resuspended...
steps.instruction: The three cell pellets were resuspended in a total volume of 50 mL artificial seawater, combined into one conical tube, and vigorously shaken to homogenize the cells.

steps.title: For a second time, the resuspended...
steps.instruction: For a second time, the resuspended cells were centrifuged for for 00:05:00 at 2000 x g and 22 °C in a swinging bucket rotor.

steps.title: The supernatant was removed as before...
steps.instruction: The supernatant was removed as before ( go to step #7.3 ).

steps.title: The pellet was resuspended in 50...
steps.instruction: The pellet was resuspended in 50 mL of artificial seawater, and the cells were homogenized by vigorous shaking.

steps.title: The cells were centrifuged for a...
steps.instruction: The cells were centrifuged for a third time for 00:05:00 at 2200 x g and 22 °C .

steps.title: Remove the supernatant as before ( go to step #7
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant as before ( go to step #7.3 ).

steps.title: Resuspend the cell pellet in 400 µL of artificial seawater
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cell pellet in 400 µL of artificial seawater. This resuspension is called the "washed cells."

steps.title: Prepare 100 µL aliquots of 50000000 cells/ml
steps.instruction: Prepare 100 µL aliquots of 50000000 cells/ml .

steps.title: Dilute 2 µL of "washed cells"...
steps.instruction: Dilute 2 µL of "washed cells" ( go to step #7.10 ) into 196 µL of artificial seawater.

steps.title: Fix the diluted cells with 2 µL of 37
steps.instruction: Fix the diluted cells with 2 µL of 37.5% formaldehyde and homogenize by vortexing.

steps.title: Pipet the fixed cells into a...
steps.instruction: Pipet the fixed cells into a fixed chamber slide and determine the cell concentration. Remember that concentration of diluted and fixed cells is a 100-fold dilution from the "washed cells." Be sure to factor that dilution into your concentration. Cells can be counted on a hemacytometer (Neubauer with brightlines) or with an automated cell counter. We recommend a Luna-FL automated cell counter. LUNA-FL (Dual Fluorescence Cell Counter, Logos Biosystems, L20001, https://logosbio.com/automated-cell-counters/fluorescence/luna-fl )

steps.title: After determining the cell concentration, dilute...
steps.instruction: After determining the cell concentration, dilute the "washed cells" to final concentration of 50000000 cells/ml and split into 100 µL aliquots. One aliquot provides enough cells for 12 nucleofections.

steps.title: Prime cells for nucleofection by degrading...
steps.instruction: Prime cells for nucleofection by degrading the glycocalyx that surrounds S. rosetta .

steps.title: Spin the 100 µL aliquots of...
steps.instruction: Spin the 100 µL aliquots of washed cells ( go to step #8.4 ) at 800 x g, °C and 22 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Gently remove the supernatant from the...
steps.instruction: Gently remove the supernatant from the cell pellet with a gel-loading pipette tip.

steps.title: Combine the priming buffer components (...
steps.instruction: Combine the priming buffer components ( go to step #6 ) to make a final priming buffer (40 mM HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 34 mM lithium citrate; 50 mM l-cysteine; 15% [wt/vol] PEG 8000; and 1 µM papain)

steps.title: Resuspend each cell pellet in 100 µL of priming buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend each cell pellet in 100 µL of priming buffer.

steps.title: Incubate cells for 00:35:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate cells for 00:35:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 10 µL of 50 mg/ml,...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of 50 mg/ml, Bovine Serum Albumin to each aliquot of primed cells for quenching proteolysis from the priming buffer.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells at 1250 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells at 1250 x g and 22 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Gently remove the supernatant from the...
steps.instruction: Gently remove the supernatant from the cell pellet with a gel-loading pipette tip.

steps.title: Resuspended each cell pell in 25...
steps.instruction: Resuspended each cell pell in 25 µL of SF Buffer ( go to step #5 ). This suspension of cells is called the "primed cells."

steps.title: Store the "primed cells" on ice while preparing nucleofection reactions
steps.instruction: Store the "primed cells" on ice while preparing nucleofection reactions.

steps.title: Deliver gene editing cargo via nucleofection
steps.instruction: Deliver gene editing cargo via nucleofection.

steps.title: Add 16 µL of ice-cold SF...
steps.instruction: Add 16 µL of ice-cold SF Buffer ( go to step #5 ) to the Sp Cas9 RNP ( go to step #4.3 ), which has a total volume of 4 µL . For reactions that use two different gRNAs, assemble each Sp Cas9 RNP separately then combine each Sp Cas9 RNP at this step. After the Sp Cas9 RNPs have been combined, add 16 µL of ice-cold SF Buffer

steps.title: Add 2 µL of the repair...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of the repair oligonucleotide template ( go to step #3.4 ) to the PCR tube with Sp Cas9 RNP and SF Buffer ( go to step #10.1 ).

steps.title: Add 2 µL of "primed cells"...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of "primed cells" (from go to step #9.10 ) to the PCR tube with Sp Cas9 RNP, SF Buffer, and the repair template ( go to step #10.2 ). This solution, which is called the "nucleofection mix," should have a total volume of 24 µL .

steps.title: Transfer the entire nucleofection mix into...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire nucleofection mix into one well of a 96-well nucleofection plate. At this point, prepare for the recovery step, by transferring the recovery buffer into a convenient vessel and setting the pipette to 100 µL .

steps.title: Pulse the nucleofection plate with the CM156 pulse
steps.instruction: Pulse the nucleofection plate with the CM156 pulse. 4D-Nucleofector Core Unit (Control system for performing nucleofection, Lonza, AAF-1002B, https://bioscience.lonza.com/lonza_bs/US/en/Transfection/p/000000000000203684/4D-Nucleofector-Core-Unit ) 96-well Shuttle Device (Add-on for Nucelofector 4d device to perform plate-based nucleofections, Lonza, AAM-1001S, https://bioscience.lonza.com/lonza_bs/US/en/Transfection/p/000000000000191639/96-well-Shuttle-Device )

steps.title: Allow membranes to reseal by resting...
steps.instruction: Allow membranes to reseal by resting cells in recovery buffer before growing cells again in media.

steps.title: Immediately after transfection, add 100 µL...
steps.instruction: Immediately after transfection, add 100 µL of ice-cold recovery buffer (10 mM HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 0.9 M sorbitol; 8% [wt/vol] PEG 8000) to each nucleofection transfection and gently mixed by firmly tapping the side of the plate.

steps.title: Allow cells to rest in recovery buffer for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Allow cells to rest in recovery buffer for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Gently mix the well in the...
steps.instruction: Gently mix the well in the nucleofection plate by pipetting up and down before transferring the entire volume in nucleofection well (the nucleofection mix plus the recovery buffer) into to 2 mL of low nutrient media in one well of a 6 well plate.

steps.title: Incubate at 22 °C, and 60% humidity for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 22 °C, and 60% humidity for 00:30:00

steps.title: Add E
steps.instruction: Add E. pacifica food and grow transfected cells.

steps.title: Add 10 µL of 10 mg/ml of E
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of 10 mg/ml of E. pacifica to the wells in the 6 well plate. Prepare the E. pacifica solution by resuspending a frozen, 10 mg pellet of E. pacifica in 1 ml of artificial seawater.

steps.title: Incubate the 6 well plate at...
steps.instruction: Incubate the 6 well plate at 22 °C, and 60% humidity for 24:00:00 before using in downstream experiments.

steps.title: Add 10 µL of 1 µg/ml...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of 1 µg/ml of cycloheximide to the 2 mL culture of transfected cells after allowing the cells to fully recover. Cycloheximide is toxic. Handle carefully and properly dispose.

steps.title: Incubate the cells in cycloheximide for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the cells in cycloheximide for 96:00:00 prior to genotyping and clonal isolation.
